user_outcome: An operator with OpenAI-compatible API keys keeps everything working through ONE hardened in-process driver behind the same contract — the agent loop and provider wire format become an implementation detail invisible to the orchestrator, and the in-process verifier role runs the tool loop before emitting its structured verdict (closing the sworn#55 gap for in-process dispatch too).
rationale: Wraps the existing internal/agent.Run tool loop and the OAI/OpenAIResponses clients as a Driver — an option, not the default. Grounding: agent.Run (internal/agent/agent.go:81) already owns exit-on-no-tools with the 25-turn cap as circuit breaker and returns the full message history; the tool executor (internal/agent/tools.go) already confines paths and sets cmd.Dir=root. The agentic-verify mechanism is NEW: run the tool loop for investigation, then obtain the verdict via one ChatStructured call over the accumulated transcript against DispatchInput.VerdictSchema — replacing today's zero-tool single-shot (verify.RunAgentic, internal/verify/verify.go:189, the sworn#55 gap) for in-process dispatch. Wrapping only: this slice does not edit internal/agent or internal/model (their cost/computeCost edits belong to S08 in T4; the depends_on edge serialises the shared package). The S27 content-always-present fix and structured plumbing (internal/model/structured.go) are consumed as-is.

effort_complexity.rationale: Moderate file count (wrap, don't rewrite), but the loop-then-structured-verdict mechanism is new and provider quirks must stay contained behind the driver.

risks.description: Reusing the agent transcript for the final ChatStructured verdict call trips provider-specific structured-output quirks (strict projection, forced-tool fallback).
risks.mitigation: The existing structured plumbing (strictProjection, normaliseStructuredContent) is reused unchanged; a structured-emission failure maps to Status=error ErrKind=protocol (fail-closed to INCONCLUSIVE at the engine), never a fabricated verdict. Covered by httptest-server tests and S10 conformance.

risks.description: Touching internal/agent or internal/model from this slice collides with T4's planned edits to the same packages.
risks.mitigation: Touchpoints are internal/driver files only; any needed capability must be achievable through the packages' existing exported surface, or the slice BLOCKs and routes back to the planner rather than silently widening its touchpoints.

acceptance_criteria.text: When Dispatch is called with Role=implementer and an OAI-compatible model, the driver SHALL execute the multi-turn tool loop (internal/agent.Run) rooted at WorktreeRoot (after the S01 AssertWorktree guard) and return Status=ok with ResultText set to the loop's final text and InputTokens/OutputTokens/DurationMS populated. TestInprocessImplementer proves it against an httptest server scripting a tool-call turn followed by a terminal turn.

When Dispatch is called with Role=verifier, the driver SHALL first run the tool loop for investigation and then obtain the verdict via exactly one ChatStructured call over the accumulated transcript against DispatchInput.VerdictSchema, returning it in Result.StructuredJSON — the in-process verifier can now re-run tests and read live repo state (sworn#55) while the ENGINE still performs verifier-verdict-v1 validation.

If the model emits a tool-only turn (empty content), the wire request SHALL still serialize a present content field (the S27 content-omitempty regression guard), asserted by a test that inspects the raw request body the driver sends.

The loop SHALL exit on no-tool-calls with the turn cap acting only as a circuit breaker; a max-turns exit SHALL map to Status=error with ErrKind=transient (retryable), and a structured-emission failure on the verifier path SHALL map to Status=error with ErrKind=protocol — never a panic, never a fabricated verdict.
This slice SHALL NOT edit internal/agent or internal/model (wrapping only — verified by the diff touching only internal/driver files); `go test ./internal/driver/...` SHALL pass.
